text:
TITLE: AISHA AND THE SANDS OF DESTINY . 
ACT 1. 
THE GILDED CAGE 
EXT. DESERT – DAWN
The sun does not rise over the Kingdom of QAZAR AL-ZAMAN.
It ignites it.
A golden ﬂare spills over endless dunes, racing toward a massive ring-wall 
of limestone that encircles the kingdom like a sacred embrace.
The morning heat touches the magical barrier atop the walls — a faint 
shimmer, like trembling glass — before it fades into perfect transparency.
Caravans approach the open gates: merchants on camels, travelers on 
foot, men and women carrying crates of spices, woven carpets, vials of 
curious herbs, preserved insects ﬂoating in liquid, and shimmering fabrics 
from distant coasts.
Their chatter ﬁlls the air — excitement, exhaustion, bragging, bargaining.
EXT. CITY GATE – CONTINUOUS
A grand wooden counter sits just inside the gate, covered with scrolls, wax 
seals, and ink pots.
Behind it sits the ENTRY CLERK — a middle-aged bureaucrat whose face 
says he’s seen everything and enjoys stamping things a little too much.
MERCHANT #1
(placing a basket forward)
“Saﬀron, cinnamon, crushed star-pepper… ﬁnest from the East!”
The Clerk sniﬀs a ﬁngerful of spice — immediately COUGHS explosively.
CLERK:
“Approved.”
STAMP .

Merchant beams and rushes past.
MERCHANT #2
(holding a jar of glowing beetles):
“These, sir, are bioluminescent—”
CLERK:
(recoiling)
“No sorcery, no bugs, no night-crawling mysteries. Denied.”
STAMP — REJECTED.
The merchant gasps, deeply oﬀended.
EXT. CITY GATE – SKY ABOVE
Suddenly—
SCREEEEEE!!
A streak cuts through the sky — ALTAIR, the Royal Hawk, feathers sleek, 
eyes sharp, carrying a sealed scroll with red wax.
He dives toward the palace.
ALTAIR:
“Make way! Royal Air Mail! Important! Urgent! Out of the way!”
Below, merchants shield their heads. The Clerk rolls his eyes.
EXT. INNER GATE – CONTINUOUS
Guards suddenly close the heavy golden doors.
Merchants outside yell in panic as the doors slam just before they enter.
Dust rises. Altair swoops over the city.
EXT. QAZAR AL-ZAMAN – FLYOVER
Altair ﬂies across the vibrant kingdom:
• narrow and wide streets lined with mosaics
• colorful markets with silks and fruits
• artisans hammering brass

• children running beneath hanging lanterns
• turquoise domes and cascading gardens
• an enormous central plaza with a mandala-like mosaic
Rising over all of this is the GREAT PALACE QAZAR AL-ZAMAN, crowned 
with turquoise domes, surrounded by lush courtyards and fountains.
Altair banks toward the tallest tower.
INT. PALACE – AISHA’S BALCONY – MORNING
AISHA (16) stands on her vast balcony overlooking the internal courtyard.
Her kaftan and headscarf dance in the light breeze.
Beside her lounges DORA the PANTHER — short white with black spots 
fur, golden collar with sun amulet, royal lazy attitude.
Aisha leans forward, eyes sparkling with longing.
AISHA:
“Look at them, Dora… today the traders bring wonders from the Edge 
Lands. Indigo, star-fruit, shells that glow in moonlight… and I cannot even 
see them up close.”
Dora stretches luxuriously on her royal pink silk cushion.
DORA:
“Aisha, Aisha… why chase the world when you can be lazy like me? Eat 
grapes, nap in
sunbeams, let politics wait another ten years.”
Aisha shoots her a look.
AISHA:
“How am I meant to rule one day if I’m not allowed beyond these walls?”
DORA:
“Because, my dear princess… you are still a child.”
Aisha stiﬀens.
AISHA:
(quiet, ﬁrm, frowning hilariously)
“I’m not.”

A moment of silence.
Then—
SCREEEEEE!
Altair zooms past the balcony, feathers wild, expression frantic.
AISHA: “Altair?”
He freezes mid-air for half a second, wings ﬂapping awkwardly.
ALTAIR:
“Royal business! Urgent! Sorry, Princess!”
(he darts away)
Aisha and Dora exchange a stunned look.
DORA:
“You see? Complicated royal things. Better left alone.”
Aisha’s eyes narrow with a spark of rebellion.
AISHA (whispering to herself, mischievous):
“…Well, opportunity does knock. Or scream.”
She takes oﬀ running.
DORA:
“AISHA—! Get back here!”
INT. ROYAL MESSENGER HALL – MOMENTS LATER
A majestic room:
arabesque arches, scroll shelves, painted tapestries depicting ancient 
history, a door open to a serene internal patio.
Altair is perched on a golden Royal Falconer’s Perch, trying to smooth his 
feathers after his chaotic ﬂight.
Aisha bursts in, slightly breathless.
Altair straightens instantly, puﬃng himself up as if he hadn’t just nearly 
collided with three domes and a laundry line.

AISHA:
“Altair! You came so fast today.”
ALTAIR:
“I bring serious news from the Borderlands. This scroll must reach the 
Sultan immediately—sealed, unread, untouched.”
Aisha gasps dramatically.
AISHA:
“Oh! But—Altair, didn’t anyone tell you? My father… he woke with a 
terrible fever.”
Altair’s eyes widen.
ALTAIR:
“The Sultan? A fever? Impossible. He—he never gets sick! —”
AISHA:
“It came suddenly. He asked me to collect all morning dispatches 
personally.. He could not even stand.”
Altair ﬂutters in distress.
ALTAIR:
“Oh dear skies… I should bring him water… or soup—”
AISHA:
“No, no. He asked me to take everything. I’ll deliver it myself”
Altair hesitates, torn.
She adds a soft, sad touch.
AISHA (super innocent eyes, pleading expression):
“He trusts you, Altair. Don’t make him walk. Pleeeease”
ALTAIR:
“Fine… but don’t break the seal! Delivery conﬁrmed!”
He drops the scroll into her hands.
Aisha beams.

INT. AISHA’S ROOM – SHORTLY AFTER
Aisha unrolls the scroll across her carpet.
Her expression shifts.
The map shows:
• Qazar al-Zaman untouched, glowing gold
• Neighboring kingdoms swallowed by dark ink clouds
• Names: Mayala, Ramlat al-Jawhar — crossed, scarred
• Notes scrawled urgently
“Shadows consume Mayala.”
“Ramlat al-Jawhar reduced to rocks.”
“Borders collapsing.”
“Royal armies required—urgent.”
“—Vizier of Outer Lands, Al-Raki Ibn Sharif”
Her breath tightens.
AISHA:
“…What happened out there?”
Suddenly—
KNOCK.
Too late to hide the broken seal.
The SULTAN enters.
Tall, old, tired eyes — a man who has carried too much loss.
SULTAN
“Aisha.”
Aisha freezes.
SULTAN:
(extends hand)
“Give me the scroll.”
She attempts a desperate smile.
AISHA:
“Oh! I was just taking it to you—”

SULTAN (ﬁrm):
“Now.”
She hands it over. The seal is broken.
His disappointment is heavier than anger.
SULTAN:
“You disobeyed. Again.
AISHA:
“I just looked! Because no one tells me anything! I’m nearly grown, Father, 
and you still—”
SULTAN (raising his voice for the ﬁrst time):
“You do not decide what you are ready for!”
Aisha ﬂinches. Dora lowers her head behind her.
SULTAN (quiet, but sharper):
“These matters are not gossip or curiosity. They are dangerous…far too 
dangerous for a child like you!”
AISHA (voice trembling with frustration):
“I am not a child!”
A long beat.
The Sultan gestures toward the towering walls beyond her balcony.
SULTAN:
“You think these walls will always protect you?
You think I will always be able to protect you?”
SULTAN (CONT’D)
(sad, bitter)
“Even I can fail!…”
Aisha stares — shaken.
AISHA:
“…What do you mean?”

He exhales, grief and fear ﬂickering.
SULTAN:
“Your mother . She was just like you .. and I couldn’t save her.”
Aisha freezes.
AISHA:
“You said she died of illness.”
SULTAN (steps back):
“And that is all you will know. For your own safety.”
He turns to leave — but pauses to scold Dora.
SULTAN:
“And you… panther of the royal line… your duty is to guard her, not 
entertain her mischief. I expect your eyes on her at all times.”
Dora shrinks.
SULTAN (over his shoulder):
“Aisha… one step outside your bounds today, and I will lock your 
chambers. Do not test me.”
He storms out.
INT. AISHA’S ROOM – MOMENTS LATER
DORA slumps dramatically onto the cushioned bench.
DORA
“Well. That went well.”
Aisha paces, ﬁre in her chest.
AISHA:
“He lied to me. About everything. And if something threatens the kingdom, 
I won’t sit and smile like a painted doll.”
She steps onto the balcony.
Below, the palace service courtyard bustles.
A woman carries a tray of fresh pistachio honey buns.

Dora sniﬀs. Pupils dilate.
AISHA (sly grin):
“…You smell that, Dora?”
DORA (defensive):
“No. Absolutely not. Don’t even—”
AISHA:
“One bun for you… one bun for me…”
Dora collapses instantly.
DORA: 
“…I hate how well you know me.”
EXT. PALACE COURTYARD – MOMENTS LATER
Dora approaches the bun seller with predatory grace.
BUN SELLER (shrieks):
“AIIIIII! THE SULTAN’S MONSTER IS AFTER MY TRAY!”
She ﬂails dramatically.
A guard tries to intervene, slipping on honey glaze and knocking into 
another guard.
Buns ﬂy like confetti.
One bun lands perfectly on a guard’s helmet.
Another sticks to Dora’s whiskers.
Dora delicately plucks one bun, sits proudly like a queen posing for a royal 
portrait.
BUN SELLER:
“GUARDS! SHE IS DEVOURING MY LIVELIHOOD!”
Guards scramble, slipping again, one holding the tray above his head 
defensively.
Dora chews with triumphant face and a big funny smile.
EXT. PALACE COURTYARD – AT THE SAME TIME

Aisha appears behind a SCARF SELLER carrying unsold scarves. The 
SCARF SELLER looks defeated after a bad sales day.
AISHA (soft, warm):
“These are lovely. May I try one?”
The woman freezes — she wasn’t prepared to speak to royalty.
SCARF SELLER:
“P-Princess… these are very simple scarves… not ﬁtting for royalty ..—”
Aisha drapes the sand-colored scarf around her head dramatically, then 
turns with a full regal pose, as if waiting for approval.
AISHA (smiling brightly):
“Not ﬁtting? Look at me. I’m magniﬁcent.”
The seller blinks twice, utterly confused but unable to argue.
SCARF SELLER
“Yes… yes, Princess. Very magniﬁcent.”
Aisha leans closer, lowering her voice in a conspiratorial whisper.
AISHA:
“I’ll take them. All of them. And as a royal favor, I’ll even help you carry 
them out. It must have been a long morning.”
The woman’s jaw drops. She squeaks instead of speaking.
Aisha picks up the basket like she’s done this her whole life, watches the 
chaotic bun scene, calculates the timing of the guards’ distraction.
They walk toward the service gate.
The guards — busy arguing with the bun seller — wave them through 
without looking.
Dora gasps from afar.
DORA:
“AISHA—?! NOOO—!”
But she is already gone.

Aisha steps into the bright marketplace — her ﬁrst taste of the outside 
world.
⸻ 
FADE OUT. 
END OF ACT 1.
ACT 2  
THE MARKET OF A THOUSAND WONDERS 
EXT. OUTER PALACE GATE — AFTERNOON
Aisha steps out of the palace walls disguised in a simple sand-colored 
servant’s shawl.
The sun hangs high. Heat ripples across stone.
She breathes in —
a mix of fear, awe… and freedom.
EXT. QAZAR AL-ZAMAN SOUK — ESTABLISHING SHOT — 
CONTINUOUS
The market explodes before her:
	 •	 Golden beams piercing through wooden slats
	 •	 Incense smoke rising like silver ribbons
	 •	 Vendors shouting, bargaining, laughing
	 •	 Children weaving between carpets in jeweled reds and deep 
indigos
	 •	 Copper lanterns clinking
	 •	 Heaps of pomegranates, ﬁgs, sea lilies, star-fruit
A world alive — loud, fragrant, chaotic.
Aisha steps into it like someone walking into sunlight for the ﬁrst time.
She brushes her ﬁngers along a ﬂuttering ribbon of crimson silk.

AISHA (whisper, breathless):
“It’s like stepping into another world…”
She nearly trips over a basket of pomegranates.
DORA ENTERS — CHAOTIC ENERGY
A distant whisper-shout:
DORA (O.S.):
“AISHA!! AISHA!! AISHA!!”
Aisha jumps.
Dora bounds toward her, tail puﬀed like a startled cat.
DORA:
“I turned around for SIX SECONDS and my princess became a fugitive!”
AISHA:
“Dora, please—”
DORA:
“He’ll turn me into a carpet! A white carpet, Aisha!
I shed too much for that! It’s undigniﬁed!”
Aisha grabs her face, hugs her tightly.
AISHA:
“Just stay with me. Just this once.”
Dora melts, sighing.
DORA (grumbling):

“…I hate that I love you.”
They walk deeper into the souk.
SONG SEQUENCE: “A THOUSANDS WONDERS”
Music rises — warm oud strings, gentle qanun, rhythmic darbuka.
Market crowds swirl into motion as Aisha and Dora step forward.
⸻
VERSE 1 — Aisha
AISHA:
Every color, every sound,
Like a story spun around—
Worlds I’ve only dreamed about,
Now bloom before my eyes.
Spices rise into the air,
Silks like sunsets everywhere,
I could wander, I could dare,
Beneath a thousand skies…
Aisha lifts a lantern, its jeweled facets casting constellations on her 
cheeks.
⸻
VERSE 2 — Dora
DORA:
I smell almonds, roasted sweet,
Pistachios beneath my feet—
This whole place is one big treat,
A feast for royal paws.

Velvet cushions, all hand-made,
Silks that never crease or fade—
Why chase trouble? I’d prefer,
A life of snacks and perfect fur.
She belly-ﬂops onto a velvet cushion, kneading it happily.
⸻
PRE-CHORUS — Aisha
Aisha tastes pomegranate seeds, eyes sparkling.
AISHA:
If this is freedom’s gentle taste,
If wonder waits beyond our gate,
Then how can I just stand and wait…
for life to pass me by?
⸻
CHORUS — Aisha & Dora (dancing in the market)
BOTH:
Wide as the sky, my heart learns to ﬂy,
Bright as the dawn where the new roads lie—
Maybe the world has been waiting for me,
A thousand wonders together to see.
⸻
VERSE 3 — Alternating
AISHA:
Lantern lights like fallen stars—
DORA:
And grilled cheese wrapped in golden jars—
AISHA (laughing):
Every path leads someplace far—
DORA:

Preferably with snacks.
AISHA:
Maybe I was meant to roam—
DORA:
“Or maybe you should turn back home—”
AISHA:
But something in my heart has grown—
DORA (softening):
And I can’t hold you back…
⸻
FINAL CHORUS
Wide as the sky, my heart learns to ﬂy,
Bright as the dawn where the new roads lie—
Maybe the world has been waiting for me,
A thousand wonders together to see.
The music fades as the sun glows warm across Aisha’s face.
EXT. SOUK — POST-SONG BEAT
The market returns to its normal soundscape.
Aisha wanders toward a stall of trinkets, glowing quietly from within.
Aisha pauses near a dusty wooden post.
A small golden cage hangs there.
Inside, a tiny white desert snake trembles under a piece of cloth.
The label reads:
“Reptile Stock – For Broth & Tonic.”
Aisha kneels.

AISHA:
“…Oh. Little one…”
The snake lifts its head weakly, voice fragile:
SNAKE (trembling):
“…I don’t want to disappear in a pot.
I haven’t even seen the moon yet…”
Aisha’s breath breaks.
AISHA:
“You… you can talk?”
SNAKE (quiet, quivering):
“I-I wasn’t born for stew… I promise I wasn’t.
I’m small, but I can be helpful. I can warn you of sandstorms.
I can smell danger before it comes…”
AISHA (gentle, touched): 
“If I open the cage…can you be really fast?
Before anyone sees you?”
The little snake musters a tiny bit of courage, lifting its head:
SNAKE (hopeful, tiny whisper):
“Please… let me stay with you.
I’ll coil small. I don’t eat much.
I’ll be quiet.
I— I can be loyal.”
Without hesitation, Aisha unlatches the cage.
The snake slips into her palm, cold and feather-soft, then curls beneath 
her shawl around her neck.

Dora returns, licking crumbs of pistachio dust oﬀ her whiskers.
DORA:
“The pistachios were acceptable.
The vendor was not.”
She squints suspiciously at Aisha.
DORA:
“…Why is your scarf… moving?”
Aisha freezes for half a second, then answers with perfect princess 
improvisation:
AISHA (innocent):
“Oh! Heat. Very… windy scarf.”
She pats the shawl ﬁrmly as the snake hides deeper.
DORA (narrowing eyes):
“…Mm-hm.”
They continue walking.
EXT. THE FAR EDGE OF THE MARKET — SUNSET
The colors thin.
Noise fades.
A ragged stall stands quiet, displaying:
	 •	 meteor fragments
	 •	 worn desert compasses
	 •	 sand-scoured manuscripts
	 •	 broken celestial tiles

A BEDOUIN in indigo robes sits cross-legged, amber eyes glinting.
Aisha approaches, drawn to him.
AISHA:
“These aren’t from our kingdom…”
His voice is deep, ancient.
BEDOUIN:
“Neither are you, Child of High Walls.”
Aisha tenses.
AISHA:
“You’ve traveled. You’ve seen the lands beyond?”
BEDOUIN:
“I have crossed deserts older than empires.
I have watched cities fall to shadow.
I have walked roads your maps have forgotten.”
Dora squints.
DORA (whisper):
“He looks like a wrapped date pastry…”
Aisha elbows her.
The Bedouin reaches into his satchel and lifts a golden ring with a round 
moonstone at its center.
It glows faintly under the last sunbeam.

BEDOUIN:
“You seek answers.
I cannot give you time…
…but I can give you a key.”
He places the ring into her palm.
BEDOUIN:
“Light awakens it.
You will understand when your path demands it.”
Aisha looks down at the ring.
Looks up—
He is gone.
Not vanished — simply gone, lost into the ﬂowing river of market people.
Dora looks up,  sniﬃng the ring suspiciously.
DORA:
“Oh wonderful.
We’ve been here one afternoon and you’re already collecting shiny cursed 
souvenirs.”
Aisha smiles faintly.
AISHA:
“Maybe it’s lucky.”
EXT. CITY STREETS — SUNSET
The sky burns orange. The souk glows with lanterns.
Aisha and Dora walk side-by-side through the crowd — unaware of how 
everything has just changed.

Aisha touches the hidden snake beneath her shawl. Then she touches the 
ring.
END OF ACT 2
ACT 3.   
ONE WISH 
INT. AISHA’S CHAMBERS – NIGHT
Moonlight spills across the marble and rose-quartz ﬂoor. The chamber is a 
treasure-box of warm lanterns, carved cedar, embroidered linens, swirling 
mosaic patterns, and a quiet desert breeze.
Aisha enters with a burst of laughter and relief, tossing her servant shawl 
aside and replacing it with her soft royal hijab.
Dora pads in behind her — tired, dramatic — and collapses onto her 
enormous velvet cushion.
Then she freezes.
Her pupils shrink.
DORA (horror-whisper):
“Aisha… do NOT… move.”
Aisha blinks.
AISHA:
“…Why?”
DORA:
“There is a SNAKE.
ON.
YOUR.
SHOULDER.”

The little white snake pokes its head out, blinking innocently.
SNAKE:
“Well hello to you too, Miss Roaring-Claws.”
Dora screams, launching three feet straight up.
DORA:
“It talks! The LIZARD talks!  THROW IT OUT OF THE WINDOW.”
SNAKE (funnily oﬀended):
“Lizard? I beg your pardon?… I am Niura, a little harmless snake.
And my fangs are purely decorative.”
Aisha bursts into laughter and gently holds them apart.
AISHA:
“Enough, both of you.
She’s our friend now.”
DORA (jealous):
“Snakes are never friends.
They slither. They plot. They don’t cuddle.”
(snarls at the snake)
“If you ever hurt Aisha, I snap. Understood?”
The snake’s green eyes narrow mischievously.
It sticks out its tongue then curls proudly on Aisha’s pillow.
Dora gasps, scandalized.
Aisha dreamingly looks at the ring on her ﬁnger  — the Bedouin’s gift.
Oval moonstone. Gold ﬁligree.
It glows faintly.
Aisha brigs it closer to her eyes.
AISHA (soft):

“He said it brings light. Truth. But how does it work?”
Dora snorts loudly.
DORA:
“By being a normal ring, Aisha.
Maybe if you rub it hard enough, poof, the a giant spirit will appear!”
She theatrically jumps oﬀ the bed and rubs her own shadow on the wall, 
making it look like a monstrous genie.
DORA (big booming voice, posing funnily):
“BEHOLD! I grant you three wishes!
Wish One: a palace made entirely of… CHICKEN!”
The snake NIURA rolls her eyes and sighs.
Aisha giggles — then pauses.
She looks closer at the moonstone. It’s swirling faintly under the stone 
surface.
AISHA:
“Maybe it needs more light?..”
She brings her candle lamp close to the ring. Nothing.
Dora laughs so hard she rolls.
SNAKE NIURA (simply, logically):
“It’s a moonstone, you know.
Maybe the light it needs is moonlight, not ﬁre.
Try to catch a moonbeam!”
AISHA freezes.
Then her eyes widen.

AISHA (whispering to herself):
“Moon… light.
Of course!”
She dashes to the balcony and extends her hand toward the moon.
The moonstone drinks the light.
A pulse.
Soft at ﬁrst — then stronger.
Pearlescent spirals bloom inside the gem.
A faint, melodic hum rises.
The air trembles. 
DORA freezes mid-shadow performance.
DORA:
“…Aisha…?
Your ring is humming.”
A ﬁlament of golden plasma slips out of the moonstone.
Then another. Then another.
They weave into a ﬂoating sphere — then elongate, shaping: arms, legs, 
torso, hair ﬂowing like molten light, a coat of drifting plasma silk, eyes 
burning gold.
A DJINN stands before them — elegant and luminous.
AISHA gasps.
DORA screams, leaping backward and knocking over half her cushion pile.
The DJINN  stretches — a cascade of glittering sparks popping oﬀ his 
joints.

DJINN (with mischievous happy smile):
“Woooo! Finally OUT!
You have NO idea how cramped that lamp is.
I’m coughing up sand from the last century.”
Dora peeks from behind a cushion, fur puﬀed like a cactus.
Aisha can barely breathe. Her eyes shine.
AISHA:
“You’re… real?…”
DJINN hovers upright, straightens his glowing collar, and grins.
DJINN:
“Real?
He ﬂoats beside.
DJINN:  
“If I grant you ONE wish, will it make me real enough for you?”
 
The snake NIURA looks funny bewildered.
SNAKE NIURA: 
 “Only one?!..”
He lifts his hand.
A glowing oud riﬀ ﬁlls the room — playful, rhythmic, desert-dance energy.
Dora groans.
DORA:
“Oh no. Oh no no—Aisha, it’s going to SING—”
And indeed:

The Djinn snaps — a ﬂoating plume of glowing calligraphy spins into 
existence.
He begins to sing.
MUSICAL NUMBER — “JUST ONE WISH”
DJINN (singing; ﬂoating backward gracefully):
“From moonlit chains and ancient plains,
your call has set me free”—
Golden dunes rise from the ﬂoor, swirling around him.
“One spark of light, one ring held tight,
and you awakened me”.
He taps the ring; constellations burst outward like ﬁreworks.
“I’ve walked with storms, shaped shifting forms,
I’ve danced on desert air”—
He becomes a silhouette made of whirling sand, then reforms.
“But one wish now, and not one more,
so choose it with great care”.
He slides past Aisha upside-down, tapping her nose.
Dora yelps and hides behind a pillow fort.
“No palace walls, no silken halls
can cage a dream that burns”—

Flashes of faraway kingdoms appear — mirage illusions.
“For one wish bold can break the mold
of fate that twists and turns".
He twirls; golden ribbons swirl, weaving shapes around Aisha.
Dora attempts to swipe them — they phase through her.
“So think not small or shy or slight,
think wild as desert breeze”—
He causes tassels, carpets, pillows to lift and dance gently.
“For one wish lifts the veils of night
and bends the world with ease”.
A glowing world map unfurls in light before Aisha.
He lands softly, bows — the illumination collapses like stardust.
Aisha claps, glowing.
Dora peers from behind a cushion, hair puﬀed to double size.
DJINN:
“So, Princess…
What’s your one and only wish?”
Aisha gathers her courage.
AISHA:
“I want to see the world.
The real one. Beyond these walls.”
The Djinn smiles — not surprised.
As if he expected exactly that.

He circles her once, ﬁngers trailing golden dust, shaping ethereal shoes 
from moonlight: semi-transparent, sapphire-edged, gold-ﬁligree lines that 
shift like constellations, tiny star-bright diamonds glowing at the arches.
He kneels as they touch the ﬂoor.
DJINN:
“Behold. The Shoes of Soaring”
Dora approaches cautiously.
DORA (awed whisper):
“…They… actually look royal.”
DJINN:
“They are.
But dormant.”
Aisha frowns.
AISHA:
“Dormant how?”
The Djinn notices the moonbeam narrowing and clicks his tongue.
DJINN (smirking):
“Oh—moon’s slipping.
My magic hates being late.”
He drifts closer, lowering his voice in a playful conspiratorial way, eyes 
glowing with mischief.
DJINN (quiet, teasing):

“A fast hint before curtain-fall…
They run on the Sands of Time.
Sand that ﬂows, yet never empties.
Find it—
and they’ll wake for you.”
The last word trails as his body dissolves—
starting at the ﬁngertips, then the hair, swirling into thin ribbons of moonlit 
smoke.
All of it funnels neatly back into the ring with a soft whump.
Silence.
Aisha rushes to the shoes.
AISHA:
“Sand. Sand… where do I get sand…”
She digs through her childhood treasure box — seashells, marbles, 
scribbled maps.
